entities.summary: GPU worker process: executes model forward passes isolated from scheduling

detail.name: ModelRunner
detail.description: Actor-pattern worker in separate process. Receives commands via shared memory, executes on GPU.
detail.why: Isolates GPU execution from scheduling — avoids GIL contention

teaches.explain: Isolated process with own state. Communicates only via messages. No shared mutable state. Eliminates race conditions.
teaches.rationale: Used here because: Isolates GPU execution from scheduling — avoids GIL contention
teaches.cross_lang: Erlang/Elixir processes, Akka actors (Java/Scala), Go goroutines+channels, Rust actix
teaches.gotcha: Unbounded mailbox/queue leads to OOM under pressure — always set capacity limits and back-pressure

teaches.explain: Python GIL prevents true parallelism in threads. Separate process sidesteps GIL entirely. Required for CPU+GPU parallelism.
teaches.rationale: Used here because: Isolates GPU execution from scheduling — avoids GIL contention

detail.design: Separate process avoids GIL; shared memory avoids serialization vs pipes
detail.convention: All GPU operations exclusively inside ModelRunner — scheduler never touches CUDA
detail.perf: CUDA graphs eliminate kernel launch overhead for decode; prefill remains dynamic

entities.summary: Load model weights, allocate KV cache, set up shared memory

detail.name: __init__
detail.description: Heavy initialization: loads checkpoint, creates model, allocates GPU buffers, connects shared memory.

teaches.explain: Do all expensive work (model loading, GPU allocation) at startup. Runtime operations are then fast and predictable.
teaches.rationale: Heavy initialization: loads checkpoint, creates model, allocates GPU buffers, connects shared memory

entities.summary: Signal model runner process to terminate

detail.name: exit
detail.description: Sends shutdown command via shared memory, waits for process cleanup.

teaches.explain: Send termination signal, let process clean up its own resources (free GPU memory, close handles). Never force-kill if avoidable.
teaches.rationale: Sends shutdown command via shared memory, waits for process cleanup

entities.summary: Main event loop: poll for commands, dispatch to handlers

detail.name: loop
detail.description: Infinite loop reading commands from shared memory. Dispatches to warmup/run/exit handlers.

teaches.explain: Infinite loop: poll for events → dispatch to handler → repeat. Foundation of servers, game engines, UI frameworks.
teaches.rationale: Infinite loop reading commands from shared memory

entities.summary: Deserialize command tensor from shared memory

detail.name: read_shm
detail.description: Reads command type and payload from shared memory buffer.

teaches.explain: Define fixed layout in shared memory: header (command type) + payload. Both processes agree on format. Zero-copy data exchange.
teaches.rationale: Reads command type and payload from shared memory buffer

entities.summary: Serialize result tensor into shared memory for scheduler

detail.name: write_shm
detail.description: Writes output tokens/logits to shared memory for scheduler to read.

teaches.explain: Write directly to shared buffer. Reader accesses same physical memory. No serialization/deserialization, no copies. Fastest possible IPC.
teaches.rationale: Writes output tokens/logits to shared memory for scheduler to read

entities.summary: Send command to model runner and wait for result

detail.name: call
detail.description: Writes command to shared memory, signals process, blocks until result ready.

teaches.explain: Write request → signal → wait for response. Looks like function call but executes in another process. Shared memory avoids copy overhead.
teaches.rationale: Writes command to shared memory, signals process, blocks until result ready

entities.summary: Run dummy forward pass to trigger CUDA lazy initialization

detail.name: warmup_model
detail.description: First CUDA call is slow (context creation, JIT compilation). Warmup pays this cost before serving.

teaches.explain: First GPU operation triggers driver init, JIT compile, memory allocation. Run dummy input at startup so first real request isn not slow.
teaches.rationale: First CUDA call is slow (context creation, JIT compilation)

entities.summary: Pre-allocate GPU memory for KV cache blocks

detail.name: allocate_kv_cache
detail.description: Allocates fixed pool of GPU tensors for KV cache. Size determined by available GPU memory.

teaches.explain: Query available GPU memory, subtract model weights, divide remainder into KV cache blocks. Fixed budget prevents OOM during serving.
teaches.rationale: Allocates fixed pool of GPU tensors for KV cache

entities.summary: Build physical block mapping tensor for attention kernel

detail.name: prepare_block_tables
detail.description: Converts logical block indices to physical GPU addresses for paged attention kernel.

teaches.explain: Build indirection table mapping logical pages to physical addresses. Kernel uses table to find correct memory locations. Like OS page tables.
teaches.rationale: Converts logical block indices to physical GPU addresses for paged attention kernel

entities.summary: Batch multiple prefill requests into single padded tensor

detail.name: prepare_prefill
detail.description: Packs variable-length prompts into one tensor with padding for batch GPU processing.

teaches.explain: Variable-length inputs padded to max length in batch. Trades compute waste (padding) for parallelism benefit. Core inference optimization.
teaches.rationale: Packs variable-length prompts into one tensor with padding for batch GPU processing

entities.summary: Batch single-token decode steps for multiple sequences

detail.name: prepare_decode
detail.description: Each sequence contributes one token. Batch together for GPU-efficient parallel decode.

teaches.explain: During generation, each sequence needs only 1 forward pass for 1 new token. Batch N sequences decode steps together for GPU efficiency.

entities.summary: Prepare sampling parameters tensor for batched sampling

detail.name: prepare_sample
detail.description: Collects temperature and top_p values for each sequence in batch.

teaches.explain: Instead of per-item loops, collect parameters into tensors. GPU applies them in parallel across the batch.
teaches.rationale: Collects temperature and top_p values for each sequence in batch

entities.summary: Execute model forward pass on prepared batch

detail.name: run_model
detail.description: Runs the actual neural network forward: embeddings → layers → logits.

teaches.explain: Feed prepared input tensors through model. Output is logits tensor. The core computation step in each engine iteration.
teaches.rationale: Runs the actual neural network forward: embeddings → layers → logits

entities.summary: Full step: prepare inputs → forward → sample → return tokens

detail.name: run
detail.description: Orchestrates one complete inference step for a batch of sequences.

teaches.explain: prepare_inputs → model_forward → sample_tokens → return_results. Each step separated for profiling and optimization.
teaches.rationale: Orchestrates one complete inference step for a batch of sequences

entities.summary: Record GPU operations into CUDA graph for zero-overhead replay

detail.name: capture_cudagraph
detail.description: Records decode forward pass into CUDA graph. Replay eliminates per-kernel CPU launch overhead.
detail.why: CUDA graphs eliminate ~50% of decode latency by removing kernel launch overhead

teaches.explain: Record sequence of GPU ops, then replay as single unit. CPU does not re-launch each kernel. Only works with fixed tensor shapes (decode, not prefill).
teaches.rationale: Used here because: CUDA graphs eliminate ~50% of decode latency by removing kernel launch overhead
teaches.cross_lang: Metal command buffers (Apple), Vulkan command buffers, DirectX command lists, OpenCL command queues
teaches.gotcha: Only works with fixed tensor shapes — dynamic shapes (variable-length prefill) cannot use graphs

detail.design: Only decode path (fixed batch size) can be graphed — prefill has variable shapes
detail.perf: Most impactful single optimization in the codebase
